subject: Ecology
title: Bloom Timing
description: A fast breakdown of Bloom Timing for builders.

hook.text: Flowers don't bloom randomly—they time their opening to match pollinator activity.

buildup.text: Early spring flowers bloom when queen bumblebees emerge hungry from hibernation.

discovery.text: Staggered blooming within a community reduces competition for the same pollinators.

twist.text: Climate change is shifting bloom times faster than pollinator emergence—creating mismatches.

climax.text: A flower that blooms a week before its pollinator arrives may never set seed that year.

punchline.text: Timing is everything when your partner has wings.

quiz.question: What happens when bloom times shift faster than pollinator emergence?
options: Flowers may not get pollinated and fail to set seed
Pollinators switch to new flowers easily
